project.name: OpenMind
project.hub_chat: This conversation is the architecture hub. All spoke chats reference this context.

owner.name: Achim Imboden
owner.role: Change management expert + AI Adoption Architect, University of Basel
owner.technical_level: Vibe coder — strong product instincts, comfortable with HTML/JS/CSS, learning backend
owner.collaborator: Sebastian (codebar) — Laravel/PHP expert, joins later phases

problem.core: Multiple active projects scattered across Claude conversations with no unified view, no persistent memory, and no way to query semantically across all content.
pain_points: Claude conversations end and context is lost
HTML tools produce outputs that go nowhere
Meeting notes never connect to projects
No portfolio view across all active work
Status always in head, never in a system

vision: A local-first, AI-native project operating system. Raw inputs flow into a Node.js orchestration server on a dedicated Mac Mini M1. Two local models run in parallel on every input: qwen2.5:3b extracts structured data, nomic-embed-text generates a semantic vector. Both write to Supabase. A static GitHub Pages dashboard reads from Supabase for exact queries and semantic search. Zero cloud inference cost. Private data stays local.
principles: Capture friction must be near zero — every input path under 60 seconds
Intelligence at ingestion, not at query time
Two query modes always available: exact SQL and semantic similarity
No cloud dependency for AI — everything runs on Mac Mini
Static frontend — no backend server beyond the Node.js orchestration server
Own every line — no OpenClaw, purpose-built Node.js server from scratch

mac_mini.role: Dedicated always-on local server — orchestration + inference only
mac_mini.usage: Headless server — not used as a personal computer
pending: launchd services for ollama + node server auto-start on boot

architecture_decisions.extraction_model: qwen2.5:3b instead of gemma3:4b — already installed, stronger JSON instruction following
architecture_decisions.orchestration: Purpose-built Node.js + Express server (~120 lines) — replaces OpenClaw entirely
architecture_decisions.remote_access: Tailscale — private mesh VPN, already installed
architecture_decisions.database: Supabase free tier — same project as existing Decision Jam sprint platform
architecture_decisions.frontend: Static HTML + Tailwind CDN on GitHub Pages — same repo as sprint platform

endpoints: POST /ingest — classify + extract + embed → write to Supabase
POST /search — embed query → pgvector cosine similarity → return top-k
GET /projects — return project list from Supabase
GET /health — Ollama status + Supabase connectivity + last ingest timestamp

database.existing_project: Same as Decision Jam sprint platform

database_schema.extension: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

tables.projects: CREATE TABLE projects (id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, code text UNIQUE, title text NOT NULL, status text DEFAULT 'active', description text, context_url text, facilitator text, updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
tables.updates: CREATE TABLE updates (id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, project_id uuid REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, raw_text text, summary text, input_type text, source text, submitted_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
tables.tasks: CREATE TABLE tasks (id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, project_id uuid REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, update_id uuid REFERENCES updates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, title text NOT NULL, status text DEFAULT 'open', due_date date, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
tables.decisions: CREATE TABLE decisions (id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, project_id uuid REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, update_id uuid REFERENCES updates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, body text NOT NULL, made_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
tables.embeddings: CREATE TABLE embeddings (id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, source_id uuid NOT NULL, source_type text NOT NULL, content text NOT NULL, embedding vector(768), created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()); CREATE INDEX ON embeddings USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 100);

database_schema.rls: Public read/write — anon key access, same pattern as sprint platform sessions table

classify_input.goal: Determine input type and which project it belongs to

output_schema.input_type: update | decision | task_list | idea | meeting_note | reflection

extract_structure.goal: Extract structured data from raw text

generate_embedding.runs: parallel with extraction jobs — not sequential

kindling_integration.decision: Keep Kindling as separate tool. Add lightweight bridge in Phase 6.
kindling_integration.bridge_mechanism: Add contact_id FK to projects table. Add project_id FK to Kindling person schema.
kindling_integration.deferred_until: OpenMind Phase 1-4 stable and running for 4-6 weeks

explicitly_deferred: Automatic Claude conversation capture (blocked by Claude.ai CSP)

existing_tools.sprint_platform: https://blackmath88.github.io/onehour-minisprint/ — same Supabase project, same repo, same design system
existing_tools.kindling: https://github.com/achimimboden/kindling — personal network visualizer, Phase 6 integration target

open_questions_remaining: tailscale status — is it authenticated and connected?
What are the 3-5 active projects to seed the database with?
Supabase project URL and anon key (needed for server .env)
